perturbation.original_text: Galacticomm must provide a person(s) to correct any technical problems (Server being down or inaccessible) 24 hours per day, 7 days per week. This person(s) must be available by beeper or telephone.
perturbation.changed_text: Galacticomm will attempt to correct technical problems in a timely manner during normal business hours.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the requirement for 24/7 technical support, a crucial service for web hosting. By stating only an 'attempt' to correct during business hours, the contract removes the guarantee of service uptime, creating a potentially significant legal contradiction, especially if downtime results in damages to Horst Entertainment. The term 'normal business hours' is also ambiguous, leading to further legal risk.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Florida Deceptive and Unfair Trade Practices Act (FDUTPA)
perturbation.law_explanation: The original text guarantees 24/7 support. The modified text, promising only an attempt at correction during business hours, might be construed as a deceptive practice under FDUTPA if Galacticomm fails to provide reasonable support, especially if that support was a key consideration for Horst Entertainment entering the agreement. The ambiguity introduced conflicts with the requirement of fair and transparent business practices.

Home > Laws > 2023 Florida Statutes > Title XXXIII > Chapter 501 > Section 204 Quick Links General Laws Conversion Table (2024) [PDF] Florida Statutes Definitions Index (2024) [PDF] Table of Section Changes (2024) [PDF] Preface to the Florida Statutes (2024) [PDF] Table Tracing Session Laws to Florida Statutes (2024) [PDF] Index to Special and Local Laws (1971-2024) [PDF] Index to Special and Local Laws (1845-1970) [PDF] Statute Search Tips 2023 Florida Statutes (Including 2023C) Title XXXIII REGULATION OF TRADE, COMMERCE, INVESTMENTS, AND SOLICITATIONS Chapter 501 CONSUMER PROTECTION Entire Chapter SECTION 204 Unlawful acts and practices. F.S. 501.204 501.204 Unlawful acts and practices. — (1) Unfair methods of competition, unconscionable acts or practices, and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any trade or commerce are hereby declared unlawful. (2) It is the intent of the Legislature that, in construing subsection (1), due consideration and great weight shall be given to the interpretations of the Federal Trade Commission and the federal courts relating to s. 5(a)(1) of the Federal Trade Commission Act, 15 U.S.C. s. 45(a)(1) as of July 1, 2017. History. — s. 1, ch. 73-124; s. 1, ch. 83-117; s. 4, ch. 85-63; s. 2, ch. 90-190; s. 3, ch. 93-38; s. 2, ch. 2001-39; s. 23, ch. 2001-214; s. 2, ch. 2006-196; s. 4, ch. 2013-207; s. 5, ch. 2015-92; s. 4, ch. 2017-155.
perturbation.justification: The original contract guarantees 24/7 technical support, whereas the modified text only promises to 'attempt to correct' problems during business hours. This omission directly contradicts the original obligation. The Law Explanation suggests this could be a deceptive practice under FDUTPA if reasonable support isn't provided. Scraped Law Snippet 1, specifically F.S. 501.204 (1), declares 'Unfair methods of competition, unconscionable acts or practices, and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any trade or commerce are hereby declared unlawful.' By removing the guaranteed 24/7 support, especially if it was a key consideration for Horst Entertainment, Galacticomm's modified text could be viewed as an unfair or deceptive act, thus creating a contradiction.
